class Solution {
public:
    string resultingString(string s) {
        vector<char> stack;
        for (char c : s) {
            stack.push_back(c);
            while (stack.size() >= 2) {
                char last = stack.back();
                char prevLast = stack[stack.size() - 2];
                if (isConsecutive(prevLast, last)) {
                    stack.pop_back();
                    stack.pop_back();
                } else {
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
        return string(stack.begin(), stack.end());
    }

    bool isConsecutive(char a, char b) {
        int numA = a - 'a';
        int numB = b - 'a';
        int diff = abs(numA - numB);
        return (diff == 1) || (diff == 25);
    }
};

class Solution {
public:
    string resultingString(string s) {
        vector<char> stack;
        for (char c : s) {
            stack.push_back(c);
            while (stack.size() >= 2) {
                char prevLast = stack[stack.size() - 2];
                char last = stack.back();
                if (isConsecutive(prevLast, last)) {
                    stack.pop_back();
                    stack.pop_back();
                } else {
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
        return string(stack.begin(), stack.end());
    }
    
    bool isConsecutive(char a, char b) {
        int numA = a - 'a';
        int numB = b - 'a';
        int diff = abs(numA - numB);
        return diff == 1 || diff == 25;
    }
};

solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    string resultingString(string s) {
        vector<char> stack;
        for (char c : s) {
            stack.push_back(c);
            while (stack.size() >= 2) {
                char prevLast = stack[stack.size() - 2];
                char last = stack.back();
                if (isConsecutive(prevLast, last)) {
                    stack.pop_back();
                    stack.pop_back();
                } else {
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
        return string(stack.begin(), stack.end());
    }
    
    bool isConsecutive(char a, char b) {
        int numA = a - 'a';
        int numB = b - 'a';
        int diff = abs(numA - numB);
        return diff == 1 || diff == 25;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses a stack to efficiently process the string in a single pass. Each character is pushed onto the stack, and after each insertion, the top two elements are checked for consecutive pairs. If found, they are removed immediately. This ensures leftmost pairs are handled first. The stack allows O(1) operations for push/pop and O(n) space. The time complexity is O(n) as each character is processed once. The main strength is linear efficiency, while the weakness is the need for additional space for the stack.
